name: Jet
description:
'''Jet''' are an Australian rock band, formed in 2001 in [[Melbourne, Victoria]]. Founded by brothers [[Nic Cester|Nic]] (vocals, rhythm guitar, piano) and [[Chris Cester]] (drums, vocals) together with [[Cameron Muncey]] (lead guitar, vocals), they were joined the following year by [[Mark Wilson (musician)|Mark Wilson]] on bass guitar. The quartet released three studio albums – ''[[Get Born]]'' (2003), ''[[Shine On (Jet album)|Shine On]]'' (2006) and ''[[Shaka Rock]]'' (2009) – before disbanding in 2012. They reformed in 2016 for a series of shows and reunion tours. They reunited for a second time in 2023, and were inducted into the [[ARIA Hall of Fame]] in November [[2023 ARIA Music Awards|that year]]. 

The band is best known for the success of ''Get Born'' and its lead single, "[[Are You Gonna Be My Girl]]". ''Get Born'' peaked at number one in [[ARIA Charts|Australia]], as well as the top 20 in the United Kingdom and top 30 in the United States. At the [[ARIA Music Awards of 2004]], the band won in six categories: [[ARIA Award for Album of the Year|Album of the Year]], [[ARIA Award for Best Group|Best Group]], [[ARIA Award for Best Rock Album|Best Rock Album]], [[ARIA Award for Breakthrough Artist – Album|Breakthrough Artist – Album]] and both [[ARIA Award for Single of the Year|Single of the Year]] and [[ARIA Award for Breakthrough Artist – Single|Breakthrough Artist – Single]] for "Are You Gonna Be My Girl". The song reached the top 20 in Australia and UK and top 30 in the US. At the [[APRA Awards (Australia)|APRA Awards]], it won Most Performed Australian Work Overseas for three consecutive years: [[APRA Music Awards of 2005|2005]], [[APRA Music Awards of 2006|2006]], and [[APRA Music Awards of 2007|2007]]. 

''Shine On'' and ''Shaka Rock'' were Australian top five albums, while their other top 20 singles were "[[Look What You've Done]]" (2004), "[[Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is (song)|Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is]]" (2006) and "[[She's a Genius]]" (2009).
